mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 60% when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to generate a positive rank IC over 3-year rolling windows, or positive return probability drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which signals near-term earnings strength and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of observations during easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in >50% of months where inflation exceeds the threshold, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative rank IC or positive return probability below 50% over rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion that directly increases corporate earnings forecasts, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.3% signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production changes are followed by positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances over a 5-year window.
